ScatterChart.heroDescription: Plot relationships between two numeric variables, edit x and y values in a table, and preview the result instantly.
ScatterChart.whyItWorksTitle: Reveal correlation, clusters, and outliers in one view.
ScatterChart.whyItWorksDescription: A scatter chart helps people see whether two numeric measures move together, split into clusters, or contain unusual points worth investigating.
ScatterChart.comparisonPoint1: Map spend vs. conversion rate, price vs. demand, or time vs. score without forcing values into categories.
ScatterChart.comparisonPoint2: Edit numeric pairs in a simple two-column table and see the scatter plot refresh as soon as the values are valid.
ScatterChart.comparisonPoint3: Adjust the title, axis labels, theme color, grid, and legend to reuse the same chart for analysis, decks, or reports.
ScatterChart.productHighlightsTitle: Built for simple x-y plotting, fast editing, and quick export.
ScatterChart.feature1Title: Edit x and y values directly
ScatterChart.feature1Description: Update numeric pairs in the built-in table so you can clean the data and refine the plot without leaving the editor.
ScatterChart.feature2Title: Stay with a simple x-y workflow
ScatterChart.feature2Description: The editor keeps the dataset focused on x and y only, which makes the scatter chart faster to fill in and easier to review.
ScatterChart.feature3Description: Change the chart title, series label, axis labels, theme color, grid, and legend from the side panel in a few clicks.
ScatterChart.feature4Description: See updates immediately while you edit, then export the current chart as a PNG for slides, docs, or status reports.
ScatterChart.useCasesTitle: Use it for correlation analysis, campaign review, product analytics, and performance research.
ScatterChart.useCase1: Compare advertising spend and conversion rate across channels or regions.
ScatterChart.useCase2: Plot product price against sales volume to see demand sensitivity.
ScatterChart.useCase3: Analyze study time vs. exam score across classes or student groups.
ScatterChart.useCase4: Review page load time vs. bounce rate to spot experience outliers.
ScatterChart.useCase5: Track risk vs. return for portfolios, funds, or investment ideas.
ScatterChart.useCase6: Compare machine settings vs. defect rate in quality-control workflows.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide1Point1: Both axes represent numeric variables rather than categories.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide1Point2: You want to inspect correlation, spread, or clusters instead of totals.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide1Point3: Outliers matter and you want them to stand out visually.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide1Point4: You want to adjust chart labels, grid, or legend quickly while keeping the plotting workflow lightweight.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide2Point1: You want to show a continuous trend over time and a line chart tells the story more clearly.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide2Point2: You only need category comparison and a bar chart is easier to scan.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide2Point3: Your audience needs exact sequential steps rather than relationship analysis.
ScatterChart.decisionGuide2Point4: The data has too few points for pattern detection or too many labels to read comfortably.
ScatterChart.faqTitle: Everything people usually ask before making a scatter chart.
ScatterChart.faq1Question: What is a scatter chart best for?
ScatterChart.faq1Answer: It is best for showing the relationship between two numeric variables, especially when you want to inspect correlation, spread, and outliers.
ScatterChart.faq2Question: What can I customize in this scatter chart maker?
ScatterChart.faq2Answer: You can edit the chart title, series label, x-axis label, y-axis label, theme color, grid visibility, and legend visibility.
ScatterChart.faq3Question: How is a scatter chart different from a line chart?
ScatterChart.faq3Answer: A scatter chart focuses on relationships between points, while a line chart focuses on ordered progression or trend over time.
ScatterChart.faq4Question: Can I edit the chart on mobile screens?
ScatterChart.faq4Answer: Yes. On smaller screens, the data editor and customize panel open in bottom sheets so you can still update the chart without leaving the page.
ScatterChart.faq5Question: Can I export the chart after editing?
ScatterChart.faq5Answer: Yes. Use the export action in the preview area to download the current scatter chart as a PNG image.
ScatterChart.faq6Question: What data fields should I prepare first?
ScatterChart.faq6Answer: Start with one x value and one y value for each point. Then add labels, title text, and styling in the editor as needed.
ScatterChart.ctaTitle: Build a scatter chart that explains the relationship fast.
ScatterChart.ctaDescription: Enter your x and y values, fine-tune the labels and styling, and use the live preview above to turn numeric pairs into a chart that is easy to review and share.
